working_on: Close Issue #133 — separate annotated-baselines from action-needed alerts at the boot persistent-warn surface.
declared_scope: Author engine/tools/scan_persistent_warn_annotations.py helper + pytests parsing the 'Persistent-warn annotation' section of tools-validate-interpretation.md. Edit engine/tools/hooks/session-start.sh to split the persistent-warn boot surface into two output lanes: action-needed (existing format, restricted to non-annotated categories) and annotated-baselines (single-line summary count + pointer). Audit and extend the Persistent-warn annotation section (current 3 entries → expected 5; add edge_evidence_empty + engine_memory_diary_read_skipped). Update engine/operations/soft-warn-lifecycle.md with an Annotated-baselines lane subsection, amend engine/adr/0042-soft-warn-lifecycle-archive-canon.md Consequences, and mirror the change in .claude/skills/session-build-lifecycle/SKILL.md + engine/operations/session-build-lifecycle.md step 2b. File 3 follow-on GitHub Issues for non-annotated saturating categories (cross_reference_broken, adr_consequences_deliverable_audit, timestamp_helper_bypass) per the Issue body's B2 framing. phase: NA-apparatus
outcome_summary: Issue #133 closed. Five commits delivered the S-0184 audit's option-B1 adjudication: (A) scan_persistent_warn_annotations.py markdown parser with 13 pytests, (B) Persistent-warn annotation section grew 3→5 entries (edge_evidence_empty promoted; engine_memory_diary_read_skipped cross-referenced), (C) session-start.sh persistent-warn surface split into action-needed + annotated-baselines lanes with helper-failure graceful degradation, (D) soft-warn-lifecycle.md / ADR 0042 / SKILL.md + Layer-1 doc step 2b updated in lockstep. Three follow-on Issues filed for action-needed categories: #143 (cross_reference_broken), #144 (adr_consequences_deliverable_audit), #145 (timestamp_helper_bypass). Empirical verification at Phase C: 3 action-needed + 4 annotated baselines matched the plan exactly.

scope_delivery.explanation: Yes — all five plan phases (helper script + tests, annotation inventory, hook split, docs landing, follow-on Issues) landed cleanly. Empirical verification at the Phase C commit matched the plan's predicted three action-needed categories + four annotated baselines exactly.

side_discoveries.reasoning: False positive — 'deferred' appears in the new ADR/ops-doc prose I authored referring to the existing 'deferred re-audit' classification status of edge_evidence_empty before its S-0196 promotion. Not a new deferral introduced by this session; descriptive prose about a category being moved OUT of the deferred-re-audit bucket.

False positive — second occurrence of 'deferred' in the same Phase B commit, also referring to the 'deferred re-audit' section name in tools-validate-interpretation.md. Same rationale as the first marker.
